bands: custom-kit equivalence guard -- the coupe and kart now spawn their PART KITS on the SAME CarDefinition (physics untouched; the kenney fused-body fallback was retired 2026-07-15). Unlike hatchkit (a separate roster twin), the fused twin here is the measured ledger itself: every assert is the docs/baseline-metrics.md final MEASURED value +/-3% (hatchkit-equivalence precedent), NOT the aspirational handling-targets band. A drift beyond +/-3% means the kit's part colliders changed physics (early bump-stop, inertia shift) -- that is a KIT bug, not a tuning item. Run per-spec: python tools/vp_test.py specs/dekenney-kit-equivalence.json --url <editor /mcp URL>. The regenerated hatch v2 kit is covered by the existing specs/hatchkit-equivalence.json (same dims, same def). TRANSIENT-YAW guard (follow-up 2026-07-13): the launch/brake/skidpad rows here are longitudinal/steady-state and CANNOT catch a compound-collider INERTIA shift (the blind spot that let the kart slalom drift silently). That guard lives on the STABLE hatch slalom in specs/hatchkit-equivalence.json (fused reference +/-3%) -- the coupe/kart slalom are too chaotic (marginal-stability fishtail, coupe DNF) to hold an equivalence tolerance, so a step-steer/slalom transient-yaw guard is anchored to the hatch where it is deterministic.

asserts.note: measured 6.94 s +/-3% (baseline-metrics final state). The 5-6 s handling-target red is a TUNING item, not this guard's business
measured 0.680 g +/-3% (measurement-profile reality, NOT the aspirational 0.95-1.05 -- same lesson as hatchkit-equivalence)
measured 18.1 m +/-3% (the 8-14 handling-target red stays a tuning item; this guard only proves the kit did not move physics)
